message:
refactor(hook): writer-injected denial output + per-protocol deny tests

Splits the existing `print_colorful_warning` and `output_denial_for_protocol`
functions into writer-injected variants so hook output can be
captured by unit tests, and adds a substantial test suite that
asserts the per-protocol invariants for every supported coding agent.

Production-shape changes:
- `print_colorful_warning(...)` is replaced by
  `print_colorful_warning_to(writer: &mut impl Write, ...)`. All the
  body's `eprintln!` calls become `writeln!(writer, ...)` (`Learn
  more`, the `dcg explain` link, the `dcg allowlist add` link, the
  blank line, and the "False positive?" footer). The previous
  `_reason: &str` parameter remains for call-site stability and is
  still ignored.
- `output_denial_for_protocol(...)` now wraps a new
  `write_denial_to(stdout: &mut impl Write, stderr: &mut impl Write,
  ...)` test seam. The wrapper is the same shape it always was — it
  just locks `io::stdout()` / `io::stderr()` and forwards. The seam
  carries the same arguments.
- Inside `write_denial_to`, the per-protocol `serde_json::to_writer`
  / `writeln!` calls now write to the injected `stdout` instead of
  the shared `handle` lock the old code held. The Codex branch
  remains a deliberate no-op on stdout (Codex 0.125.0+ requires exit
  code 2 + stderr only — the old comment warning about `deny_unknown_fields`
  rejecting the JSON envelope is condensed but preserved).

New tests (P1.1 — Codex coverage and per-protocol regression):
- `test_write_denial_claude_produces_valid_json_on_stdout`: verifies
  ClaudeCompatible deny round-trips through serde, with the right
  `permissionDecision`, `hookEventName`, `ruleId`, `packId`,
  `allowOnceCode`, and that stderr received the colored warning.
- `test_write_denial_codex_produces_empty_stdout`: verifies the
  Codex branch writes zero bytes on stdout (regression coverage for
  the silent-deny bug that the path comment describes) while still
  writing the command and reason to stderr.
- Equivalent Copilot and Gemini tests assert their JSON shapes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
